steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage covers two related ideas: (1) Black journalism in the US has a rich history, and (2) the National Association of Black Journalists preserves and honors that history. The main idea must connect both — not fixate on one detail or introduce claims not in the text.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Cunningham's achievements are often overlooked in countries other than the United States.': the passage says nothing about other countries or about Cunningham being overlooked. ✗
  • Choice B — 'The National Association of Black Journalists was founded in Washington, DC, in the 1900s.': a supporting detail, not the main point of the passage. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Black journalism in the United States has an important history that the National Association of Black Journalists helps to honor.': captures both the significance of Black journalism's history and the NABJ's role in preserving it. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Black journalism has an interesting history both inside and outside the United States.': the passage focuses only on the US; "outside the United States" is unsupported. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
